steps.title: Find the median position
steps.content: Add the frequencies in the number-of-days column: $$3+3+4+1+3+9=23.$$ With $23$ observations, the median is the $$\frac{23+1}{2}=12\text{th}$$ observation.

steps.title: Use cumulative counts
steps.content: The first $3$ observations are $14$ birds, observations $4$ through $6$ are $15$ birds, observations $7$ through $10$ are $16$ birds, and observation $11$ is $17$ birds. The next row, $18$ birds, covers observations $12$ through $14$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The 12th observation is $18$, so the median number of birds is 18.
